title: The New Energy System Takes Shape
text:
The New Energy System Takes Shape

AJ Cortese, Arthur Kroeber

Gavekal Technologies: New Energy | 6 Jul 2026

China’s latest five-year plan for energy disappointed many analysts because of its unambitious targets for scaling back fossil fuel use. This misses the point. The plan is important because for the first time, Beijing is formally committing to an energy system based mainly on non-fossil fuels, with traditional coal, oil and gas as backup stabilizers. Achieving this goal will take another couple of decades, but policymakers are already starting to look beyond brute additions of renewables capacity to the efficiency measures that will be required for success. The core of the new energy system is the world’s most aggressive electrification drive, which is intended to enhance China’s energy security and self-sufficiency, and cement its already formidable position in advanced manufacturing.Also in this issue of New Energy Insight:
